Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Join George Vondriska to learn how to create a set of modular planter boxes that serve dual purpose as an inviting bench. Using some universal furniture making techniques, you’ll learn how to guarantee uniform length when crosscutting multiple pieces and how to ensure symmetrical part layout. You'll also learn about choosing the correct glue, material, and fasteners for outdoor projects, and lots more.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Modular Planter Bench

Video.summary: -Cordless drills are a staple in many woodworking shops and deserve a dedicated storage stand. George Vondriska shows you how to make your own cordless drill stand, fully equipped with a power strip for easy charging, plus storage drawers for tips and bits. This project is great for practicing biscuit joinery and hand tool work.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Cordless Drill Stand

Video.summary: Everything old is new in the hands of the modern quilter. Pattern designer Elizabeth Dackson reinterprets vintage blocks using a modern aesthetic. Brenda Ratliff and Heather Grant trade tips on booking the faculty for quilt events. Modern quilt enthusiast Elizabeth Dackson uses half-square triangles to create dynamic blocks.
Video.title: Old and New

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: A common thread for all artists is their relationship with color. Sometimes its bold, sometimes it's muted and other times it's noticeably absent. We begin with favorite beads inspired by Van Gogh and a bold look at color. Then artist Kate Richbourg presents an artwork color study – laddering with thread. We finish up with artist Aja Vaz and creating hollow glass beads in a multitude of colors.
Video.title: The Artist's Relationship with Color

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Artist's Academy presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Machine quilting isn't always as easy as it looks, but anyone can do these three free-motion quilting designs (promise)! Learn how wavy lines, starburst designs, and simple cursive words can turn your quilt into a work of art.
Video.title: Bonus: Top 3 Free-Motion Quilting Designs Even Beginners Can Do

Image.alt: Bonus: Top 3 Free-Motion Quilting Designs Even Beginners Can Do
Bonus: Top 3 Free-Motion Quilting Designs Even Beginners Can Do
Bonus: Top 3 Free-Motion Quilting Designs Even Beginners Can Do

Video.summary: Angela’s not the only Midnight Quilter in the Walters' house! Watch as she and her teenage daughter Cloe spend some quality time together making an easy-to-piece charm quilt.
Video.title: Like a Charm

Video.summary: All quilters can get stuck in a creative rut, even Angela. This week, she turns to strip piecing and her fun, easy-to-piece chevron pattern to get her quilting mojo back. Plus, she'll show you how to use negative space to bring your quilt to life.
Video.title: Strip-Pieced Contemporary Chevron Quilt

Video.summary: This practical and cute quilted zipper pouch is one of Angela's favorite projects yet, because it skips the piecing and gets straight to the quilting! In this episode, she shares three quilting designs every quilter should know: ribbon candy, serpentine lines and the paisley feather, while making a small, easy quilting project for beginners that only requires two fat quarters to make!
Video.title: 3 Designs Every Quilter Should Know

Video.summary: Angela demonstrates the no-waste flying geese method while making a stunning multidimensional quilt for the most "quilt-worthy" person in her life. And she shares her top 3 tips for quilting big quilts on a small machine.
Video.title: No-Waste Flying Geese Quilt

Video.summary: It seems like only yesterday that Angela was stitching a baby quilt for her youngest daughter. Tonight, she's making a flower quilt for her daughter's new big girl bed. Power to the flower!
Video.title: Flower Power Kids Quilt

Video.summary: CAUTION: Baby quilts are a total gateway drug! Just ask Angela: she's working this cute little number up for her newborn nephew. (Think you can stop at one? Don't say we didn't warn you!)
Video.title: Z Is for Zoo

Video.summary: Are you an organized quilter or a bit on the messier side like Angela? Watch as she uses solid colors to make a tried-and-true rainbow quilt by Monique Jacobs, sharing her best organizing tips along the way.
Video.title: Tried and True Rainbow Quilt

Video.summary: Have you ever worked on a quilt that seemed intimidating, but wasn't that bad once you got into it? Watch as Angela checks this snazzy Snail's Trail quilt from her quilting bucket list.
Video.title: Snail’s Trail Quilt

Video.summary: Easy piecey! This week Angela's keepin' it fun and fresh with a mod triangles quilt that's simple to put together.
Video.title: Mod Triangles Quilt

Video.summary: Are you an organized quilter or a bit on the messier side like Angela? Watch as she uses solid colors to make a tried-and-true rainbow quilt by Monique Jacobs, sharing her best organizing tips along the way.
Video.title: Tried and True Rainbow Quilt

Video.summary: If you're new to FMQ, quilting stencils are a great way to start!
Video.title: Bonus: Tips for Using Quilting Stencils

Image.alt: Bonus: Tips for Using Quilting Stencils
Bonus: Tips for Using Quilting Stencils
Bonus: Tips for Using Quilting Stencils

Video.summary: Star quality is guaranteed when accessories are in the limelight! Kay Whitt is up first with a circular jewelry pouch. Then meet Joanne Hillestad as she makes a unique tablet cover styled with quilted fabric and coordinating prints. Learn to mix and match prints for maximum impact.
Video.title: Accessories with Star Quality

Video.summary: In the 1980's, designers took their cues from active wear, workout clothes and easy care fabrics. Angela Wolf adds a sassy peplum to a basic t-shirt to add swing and style. Cindy Hogan uses the latest digital cutting technology to create appliques for a go-everywhere denim jacket.
Video.title: Fashion on the Go - the 1980's

Video.summary: Stretch it out with clay. Polymer clay artist Syndee Holt creates a pendant with sparkle clay and adds an artistic touch with transfers and illustrations. To make a necklace, she recreates the Asian art of raku with clay and mica powders. Jewelry artist Anie Piliguian buffs clay elements to a sparkling finish.
Video.title: Clay Workout

Video.summary: This season is all about learning from the masters but we'd be remiss if we didn't also take time to feature some modern ingenuity. Katie takes a look at beads inspired by Monet as a nod to the past. Then Aja Vaz brings us into the current world with modern torches and making tube shaped beads. Then Katie is back with old meets new, as we look at new ways to set crystals. Finally Tammy shows us new and old loom types.
Video.title: Modern Ingenuity

Video.summary: It's fun to take and share selfie photos. Amy Tan gets selfies out of the camera and onto artful pages. Ashley Horton uses fancy frames to capture a bit of herself in her designs.Amy Tan has a tip for adding stitches to patterned paper. Julie Fei-Fan Balzer creates a fun photo series from every day activities.
Video.title: Episode 9: Me, Me, Me

Video.summary: Adding new details keeps things fresh. Christine Drumheller adds texture as she designs with thread using the sewing machine. Amy Tan sprays and spatters paint for special effects. Julie Fei-Fan Balzer wraps it all up with a review of the fresh new techniques featured throughout the series.
Video.title: Details

Video.summary: A popular artistic trend features old, aged or antiqued looks. There are so many ways to alter the look of a material or design to give it the patina of yesteryear. First host Julie Fei Fan Balzer demonstrates a technique for giving stenciling an aged look. Then Mary Hettmansperger has a tutorial on how to rust, distress, or age just about any material from metal to fabric. Finally, Candie Cooper embosses and distresses leather to make a key fob.
Video.title: How to Make Things Look Aged

Image.alt: How to Make Things Look Aged
How to Make Things Look Aged
How to Make Things Look Aged

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Baking & Cooking

Video.summary: Beat single sock syndrome with a little multitasking! The girls go over how to knit two socks at once so you'll never end up with solo socks again.
Video.title: Multitasking for Knitters: Two-at-a-Time Socks

Video.summary: Since when do knitters use crochet hooks? Find out how to work the provisional cast-on, cable cast-on, and more during the first Novel-T check in!
Video.title: Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline

Image.alt: Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline
Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline
Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline

Video.summary: Look beyond the sky. Jenny Barnett-Rohrs makes a glittery star catcher featuring the constellations. Candie Cooper creates a 3-D board illustrating moon phases. Artist Franz Spohn creates watercolor paintings featuring clouds. Jenny makes a suncatcher mandala and Candie Cooper captures a sparkly space galaxy in a bottle.
Video.title: Stars

Video.summary: Let's discover the world we live in and beyond. Katie and Jennie create a 3-D pop-up map of the planets together, and Franz explores the artistic use of dimension and space. Gather leaves, sticks and stones and then Jenny shows how to create a nature journal. She and Katie use water and paint to create a new fish species in silhouette. Katie blasts off with a jet pack made from recycled bottles and supplies from the Family Craft Box.
Video.title: Discover the World

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: Quilt Designs: Blocks, Settings and Borders Class
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Cook & Create
